projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: [living_room] A person is moving through the house and a doorbell chime was heard.
[front_yard] The camera feed is experiencing intermittent signal loss and displaying error messages.
[front_yard] The front yard camera has lost signal.
[front_yard] The camera feed lost signal, displaying an error message.

The 16 connected devices at 06:22 confirm the family is still home, invalidating the 'dogs alone' flag, while the simultaneous multi-zone perimeter audio, doorbell triggers, urgent barking, and concurrent camera errors strongly indicate either a weather event or a persistent perimeter presence currently obscured by signal interference. This pattern warrants a quick weather check or camera reboot to ensure no genuine security or environmental issue is being masked while the household sleeps.
Dogs appear to be home alone — no family member sighted in 4.1 hours (last seen ~2:21AM). Dogs are active in camera observations.

The simultaneous doorbell trigger, repeated unclassified perimeter audio, and urgent dog barking—despite all cameras showing empty areas—strongly indicate a weather event or an animal just outside the camera's field of view is triggering the doorbell and agitating the dogs. This cross-signal pattern confirms no human activity is present and explains the early morning restlessness, suggesting a routine weather check rather than a security concern.
[front_yard] A momentary error occurred in the camera stream (Frame 2).
[dog_run] A momentary error message was visible on the camera stream.

The urgent dog barking, repeated unclassified perimeter audio, and wind-triggered doorbell alerts coincide with active camera failures on the backyard and dog_run feeds, indicating the dogs are reacting to external stimuli (likely wildlife or weather) in blind spots rather than a person or vehicle. With all 18 devices still inside and the SUV parked, there is no security threat, but Steve should be aware the cameras are currently blind to the source of the agitation.
